text: Planning a trip is always exciting, but it can also be challenging. When I visited Japan last year, I spent weeks researching places to stay, train passes, and must-see sights. Once there, I was amazed by the new culture—temples, bowing greetings, and quiet respect in public. The food was incredible: steaming ramen, fresh sushi, and sweet mochi. However, language barriers made simple tasks like ordering coffee tricky. I often used hand gestures and a translation app. Despite the difficulties, those challenges became part of the adventure. Now, looking back, the memories of smiling strangers and beautiful sunsets are priceless—proof that traveling opens your mind in unforgettable ways.
